figures.caption: Fig. 2: Learning curves over training blocks. Node Recall and Episodic Return are evaluated on the held-out FloorPlans 28–30 every 50 blocks, while Move Success Rate is computed on the training scenes after each block; shaded areas denote the 95% confidence interval across seeds. SH/MH: single-head/multi-head policy; D: depth input; CL: curriculum learning; IL: imitation learning.

Table 9: Hyperparameters for training and reward shaping. PPO entries listed as “SH16 / SH504 / MH” correspond to the compact single-head action space (16 actions), the full single-head action space (504 actions), and the multi-head factorised action space (24 rotation angles, 21 lengths, Stop), respectively.
Table 8: Feature dimensions and architecture specifications.

Fig. 5: Representative trajectories on held-out scenes (FloorPlans 28–30). Numbers in parentheses in the legends report (steps | score). All plotted scenarios except the Baseline are with depth input.
Table 7: Optuna hyperparameter search spaces. All parameters were optimised over categorical candidate sets. The optimisation target is the composite objective in Eq. 16.

Fig. 4: Representative trajectories on evaluation scenes (FloorPlans 28–30). Legends report episode length and Node Recall for the shown episodes. All plotted scenarios except the Baseline use depth input.

Table 1: Final held-out performance on FloorPlans 28–30 using the final training checkpoint of each run (means across seeds; Node Recall reported as mean ± std). Node Recall : fraction of scene graph nodes recalled at episode end. Episodic Return: cumulative reward under the composite reward function. Episode Length: number of steps taken until episode termination. Move Success Rate: fraction of executed translation actions that succeed without collision. Path Length: total path length traversed during the episode. ↑ indicates that higher values are better.
Fig. 3: Evaluation scatter plot relating traversed path length to Node Recall and episode length; each point represents one trained run (one seed) at the final evaluation checkpoint.

Table 2: Overview of ablation scenarios. IL: imitation learning pretraining. Actions: single-head parameterisation (either compact 16 actions or enlarged 504 actions) versus factorised multi-head parameterisation (Rotation angle, Length, Stop) at 504 resolution. Depth w/ Coll.-Aux : depth input with an auxiliary head for collision prediction. CL: curriculum learning with staged action-space expansion.

Fig. 1: Overview of the navigation model architecture used in our learning setup: multimodal encoders → LSTM policy core → action heads (single-head or multihead) and optional collision auxiliary head.
